meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: No SaaS Knowledge Needed
content.seo_description: Stop guessing your SaaS pricing. Learn a repeatable, expert-led workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch profitable pricing models for your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your pricing. The secret lies in value-based tiering mapped to your user personas. By using Antigravity to scaffold your subscription logic and Claude Code to automate the implementation, you can ship professional, conversion-optimized SaaS pricing modules in hours, not weeks—even without prior SaaS architectural experience.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank screen, a client deadline looming, and the crushing realization that you don't know how to architect a scalable SaaS pricing model. The industry standard feels like a black box, and every tutorial you find is either too academic or dangerously simplistic. You need to deliver value, but you're stuck in the 'no-knowledge' trap.

Relax. The era of manual billing configuration and fragile database schemas is over. We are going to bypass the complexity by leveraging AI-driven infrastructure. With Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding and Claude Code’s surgical coding capabilities, you can turn a pricing strategy into a production-ready feature set before your coffee gets cold.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Identify three core personas (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) and map them to specific feature gates. This ensures your pricing is logical before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Create a JSON schema for a three-tier pricing model (Starter, Pro, Enterprise). Include fields for monthly_price, annual_price, and a features array. Ensure the structure is compatible with Stripe API metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a pricing-schema.json file based on the architect output and place it in the /config directory.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate logic that handles tier transitions and feature access controls, preventing common bugs in subscription upgrades.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript service class named 'SubscriptionService' that handles upgrade/downgrade logic, checking the user's current plan against the pricing-schema.json.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'npm install stripe && npx generate-service SubscriptionService'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Implement the checkout flow using Claude Code to ensure secure session creation, which is the industry standard for SaaS reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe checkout session handler that accepts a price_id and user_id, returning a checkout URL. Include error handling for invalid price IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit src/api/checkout.ts 'Implement the stripe session creation logic provided by the architect.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that restricts access to premium features based on the user's active subscription status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js middleware function that checks if a user has access to a specific 'feature_key' based on their current subscription tier stored in our database.

steps.title: Automate Testing Cycles
steps.description: Verify your pricing logic with automated tests to ensure no user can access premium features without payment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Vitest unit tests for the SubscriptionService class, covering scenarios for free users, pro users, and expired subscriptions.

pro_tips: Always use 'usage-based' billing for your top-tier to capture high-value clients who outgrow fixed pricing.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database to prevent immediate lockout during payment failures, increasing customer retention.
Use hidden 'Admin' pricing tiers for custom enterprise deals to avoid cluttering your public-facing pricing page.
Always store price IDs as environment variables to allow seamless switching between Sandbox and Production without code changes.
Sync your pricing model with your database schema early to avoid 'Migration Hell' when you eventually scale to thousands of users.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing module to a staging environment, run a sandbox checkout test, and then integrate your specific product features into the gatekeeping middleware.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a senior architect. Every day you spend writing manual billing code is a day your competition is winning. Master these tools, scale your output, and dominate your niche. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
